question_text: Explain why the smell of perfume spreads throughout a room even though no one is using a fan. Use the particle model and describe both the random motion of perfume particles and their interaction with air particles.
correct_answer: Perfume particles evaporate and undergo Brownian motion—they move randomly in all directions by constantly colliding with fast-moving air particles. These random collisions gradually spread the perfume particles throughout the room over time. Although each collision is random, statistically there are more movements toward empty space, so the smell gradually fills the room.
explanation: This combines diffusion and Brownian motion: perfume particles are bombarded by air particles from all directions, causing random motion that gradually disperses them. It shows how the particle model explains everyday observations—particles don't deliberately move away; random collisions statistically spread them out.
